---
title: "Industrial Project V1"
author: "Musa Tariq"
date: "2026-03-05"
output: html_document
---


EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSIS

1. Installing/Loading Data
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
install.packages("spatstat.data")
install.packages("spatstat.geom")
install.packages("spatstat.model")
```


```{r}
library(here)
library(spatstat.model)
library(tidyverse)
library(spatstat.data)
library(spatstat.geom)
data("murchison")
```


2. Initial Inspection
```{r}

str(murchison)
dim(murchison)
summary(murchison)
head(murchison)

```
3. Basic analysis

```{r}
summary(murchison$gold)

summary(murchison$greenstone)


```


```{r}

class(murchison$gold)
class(murchison$faults)
class(murchison$greenstone)
```

6. Spatial EDA (VERY IMPORTANT FOR MURCHISON DATA)
```{r}
library(sp)
library(ggplot2)
```


```{r}
plot(murchison$greenstone, col = "lightgreen", main = "Murchison Region")

plot(murchison$faults, add = TRUE)

plot(murchison$gold, add = TRUE, pch = 20, col = "red")

```
```{r}
plot(murchison$greenstone, col = "lightgreen", main = "Murchison Greenstone")
plot(murchison$faults, main = "Murchison Fault Lines")
plot(murchison$gold, main = "Murchison Gold")
```



```{r}

# Perform discrete (or pixelated) logistic regression of point patterns
mur <- murchison
mur$dfault <- distfun(mur$faults)
slrm(gold ~ dfault, data=mur)
slrm(gold ~ dfault + greenstone, data=mur)
slrm(gold ~ dfault, data=mur, splitby="greenstone")


```





```{r}
#Differing Pixel Sizes
mur <- murchison
mur$dfault <- distfun(mur$faults)

fit_slrm_10  <- slrm(gold ~ dfault + greenstone, data = mur, eps = 10000)
fit_slrm_5   <- slrm(gold ~ dfault + greenstone, data = mur, eps = 5000)
fit_slrm_2.5 <- slrm(gold ~ dfault + greenstone, data = mur, eps = 2500)
fit_slrm_1   <- slrm(gold ~ dfault + greenstone, data = mur, eps = 1000)

coef(fit_slrm_10)
coef(fit_slrm_5)
coef(fit_slrm_2.5)
coef(fit_slrm_1)

```


```{r}
# slrm doesn't take offsets directly, so use the offset argument via a workaround
# The offset is log(pixel area) = log(eps^2)
# One approach: fit ppm() which handles this natively (see step 3)
```

```{r}
library(spatstat.model)
# PPM Gold Standard
dfault_im     <- as.im(mur$dfault, W = mur$gold$window)
greenstone_im <- as.im(mur$greenstone, W = mur$gold$window, value = TRUE, na.replace = FALSE)

fit_ppm <- ppm(mur$gold ~ dfault + greenstone,
               covariates = list(dfault     = dfault_im,
                                 greenstone = greenstone_im))
coef(fit_ppm)

lambda_ppm <- predict(fit_ppm, type = "intensity")
plot(lambda_ppm, main = "Fitted Gold Deposit Intensity — PPM (Berman-Turner)", col = heat.colors(256))
plot(mur$gold, add = TRUE, pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "blue")
```

```{r}
# Here we have the plots compared to one another
# Build results table from fitted models directly
results_df <- data.frame(
  eps         = c(10, 5, 2.5, 1, 0),
  intercept   = c(coef(fit_slrm_10)[1], coef(fit_slrm_5)[1], coef(fit_slrm_2.5)[1], coef(fit_slrm_1)[1], coef(fit_ppm)[1]),
  beta_dfault = c(coef(fit_slrm_10)[2], coef(fit_slrm_5)[2], coef(fit_slrm_2.5)[2], coef(fit_slrm_1)[2], coef(fit_ppm)[2]),
  beta_green  = c(coef(fit_slrm_10)[3], coef(fit_slrm_5)[3], coef(fit_slrm_2.5)[3], coef(fit_slrm_1)[3], coef(fit_ppm)[3])
)

# Plot - Intercept
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = intercept)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[1], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  labs(title = "Intercept (α) across pixel sizes", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "α") +
  scale_x_reverse()

# Plot - Beta dfault
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = beta_dfault)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[2], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  labs(title = "β (distance to fault) across pixel sizes", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "β") +
  scale_x_reverse()

# Plot - Beta greenstone
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = beta_green)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[3], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  labs(title = "γ (greenstone) across pixel sizes", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "γ") +
  scale_x_reverse()

```



```{r}
#THIS IS THE LAMBDA STUFF, FITTING THE MODELS TO SEE THEIR INTENSITY CHARTS

# Intensity surface for each pixel size
lambda_10  <- predict(fit_slrm_10)
lambda_5   <- predict(fit_slrm_5)
lambda_2.5 <- predict(fit_slrm_2.5)
lambda_1   <- predict(fit_slrm_1)

# Plot each one
plot(lambda_10,  main = "Fitted Intensity — SLRM 10km", col = heat.colors(256))
plot(mur$gold, add = TRUE, pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "blue")

plot(lambda_5,   main = "Fitted Intensity — SLRM 5km", col = heat.colors(256))
plot(mur$gold, add = TRUE, pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "blue")

plot(lambda_2.5, main = "Fitted Intensity — SLRM 2.5km", col = heat.colors(256))
plot(mur$gold, add = TRUE, pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "blue")

plot(lambda_1,   main = "Fitted Intensity — SLRM 1km", col = heat.colors(256))
plot(mur$gold, add = TRUE, pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "blue")

plot(lambda_ppm, main = "Fitted Gold Deposit Intensity — PPM (Berman-Turner)", col = heat.colors(256))
plot(mur$gold, add = TRUE, pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "blue")


```



```{r}
# In this one I'll attempt to split it into 100 segments going from 0 to 3000, as we've seen that ~2.5km is near ppm (ppp model) standards
eps_seq <- seq(1000, 3000, length = 100)

results_list <- list()

for (eps in eps_seq) {
  fit <- slrm(gold ~ dfault + greenstone, data = mur, eps = eps)
  results_list[[as.character(eps)]] <- coef(fit)
}

# Build results table
results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results_list), function(eps) {
  data.frame(
    eps         = as.numeric(eps) / 1000,  # convert back to km for display
    intercept   = results_list[[eps]][1],
    beta_dfault = results_list[[eps]][2],
    beta_green  = results_list[[eps]][3]
  )
}))

print(results_df)
```

```{r}
# Plotting the data but for the 100 models between 1 and 3km
# Plot 1 - Intercept
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = intercept)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", col = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[1], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  scale_x_reverse() +
  labs(title = "Intercept (α) across pixel sizes", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "α")

# Plot 2 - Beta dfault
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = beta_dfault)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", col = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[2], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  scale_x_reverse() +
  labs(title = "β (distance to fault) across pixel sizes", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "β")

# Plot 3 - Greenstone
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = beta_green)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", col = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[3], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  scale_x_reverse() +
  labs(title = "γ (greenstone) across pixel sizes", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "γ")
```

```{r}
library(pROC)

get_roc <- function(fit) {
  probs <- fit$Fit$FIT$fitted.values
  obs   <- fit$Fit$FIT$y
  roc(obs, probs)
}

roc_10  <- get_roc(fit_slrm_10)
roc_5   <- get_roc(fit_slrm_5)
roc_2.5 <- get_roc(fit_slrm_2.5)
roc_1   <- get_roc(fit_slrm_1)

# Plot
plot(roc_10,  col = "blue",   main = "ROC Curves by Pixel Size")
plot(roc_5,   col = "green",  add = TRUE)
plot(roc_2.5, col = "orange", add = TRUE)
plot(roc_1,   col = "red",    add = TRUE)
legend("bottomright",
       legend = c("10km", "5km", "2.5km", "1km"),
       col    = c("blue", "green", "orange", "red"),
       lwd    = 2)

cat("AUC 10km: ",  auc(roc_10),  "\n")
cat("AUC 5km: ",   auc(roc_5),   "\n")
cat("AUC 2.5km: ", auc(roc_2.5), "\n")
cat("AUC 1km: ",   auc(roc_1),   "\n")
```
```{r}

eps_seq <- seq(1000, 3000, length = 100)  # in metres
results_list <- list()
roc_data_list <- list()

for (pix in eps_seq) {
  fit <- slrm(gold ~ dfault + greenstone, data = mur, eps = pix,
              ruleAtPoints = "r")
  results_list[[as.character(pix)]] <- coef(fit)
  roc_data_list[[as.character(pix)]] <- list(
    probs = fit$Fit$FIT$fitted.values,
    obs   = fit$Fit$FIT$y
  )
  saveRDS(results_list, "results_checkpoint.rds")
  saveRDS(roc_data_list, "roc_checkpoint.rds")
}
```


```{r}
# Build results table
results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results_list), function(pix) {
  data.frame(
    eps         = as.numeric(pix) / 1000,  # convert back to km
    intercept   = results_list[[pix]][1],
    beta_dfault = results_list[[pix]][2],
    beta_green  = results_list[[pix]][3]
  )
}))

# Plot 1 - Intercept
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = intercept)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", col = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[1], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  scale_x_reverse() +
  labs(title = "Intercept (α) across pixel sizes - Rule R", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "α")

# Plot 2 - Beta dfault
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = beta_dfault)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", col = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[2], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  scale_x_reverse() +
  labs(title = "β (distance to fault) across pixel sizes - Rule R", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "β")

# Plot 3 - Greenstone
ggplot(results_df, aes(x = eps, y = beta_green)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", col = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = coef(fit_ppm)[3], linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  scale_x_reverse() +
  labs(title = "γ (greenstone) across pixel sizes - Rule R", x = "Pixel size (km)", y = "γ")


```
